zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Aubrey de Vere 10th Earl of Oxford於大約1338年出生在英國艾可卡素 丁加姆時，他的父親John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford27歲，母親Maud de Badlesmere32歲。他和Alice FitzWalter至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1400年2月15日在英國英格蘭奧可福爾奧可福德享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可哈德利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, mostly from Vair in Ancenis, named with Gaulish ver(n)- ‘alder’. This was the probable home in the 11th century of the Breton lord Aubrey (Albericus) de Ver, who followed William the Conqueror to England and founded an influential family who held the earldom of Oxford until 1703. In a few cases the surname could be from other places in France, such as Ver in Manche and Calvados, which have the same etymology as Vair.
French (mainy Vendée; Veré): variant of Vairé, from Old French vairie ‘variegated, spotted’, applied as a nickname for someone who has freckles.

nameMeanings.name: Aubrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French form of the Germanic name Alberic, from alb ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ric ‘power’. This was the name, according to Germanic mythology, of the king of the elves. The native Old English form, Ælfrīc, borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury, did not long survive the Conquest. Aubrey was a relatively common given name during the Middle Ages, but later fell out of favour. Its occurrence since the 19th century may in part represent a transferred use of the surname derived from the Norman given name, as well as a revival of the latter. In the United States, this is mainly used as a girl's name, perhaps under the influence of Audrey .

variants.name: Aubree
meanings: From a Norman French form of the Germanic name Alberic, from alb ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ric ‘power’. This was the name, according to Germanic mythology, of the king of the elves. The native Old English form, Ælfrīc, borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury, did not long survive the Conquest. Aubrey was a relatively common given name during the Middle Ages, but later fell out of favour. Its occurrence since the 19th century may in part represent a transferred use of the surname derived from the Norman given name, as well as a revival of the latter. In the United States, this is mainly used as a girl's name, perhaps under the influence of Audrey .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Aubrey de Vere 10th Earl of Oxford was born about 1338, in Castle Hedingham, Essex, England, his father, John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford, was 27 and his mother, Maud de Badlesmere, was 32. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Alice FitzWalter. He died on 15 February 1400, in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Hadleigh, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.hedington castle, Essex, England: Castle, Jackson, Dakota du Sud, États-Unis
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Aubrey de Vere 10th Earl of Oxford est né environ 1338, à Castle Hedingham, Essex, Angleterre, son père, John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Maud de Badlesmere, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Alice FitzWalter. Il est décédé le 15 février 1400, à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Hadleigh, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hatte mit Alice FitzWalter mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 15. Februar 1400 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hadleigh, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Aubrey de Vere 10th Earl of Oxfordが約1338年にイギリスエセックスカストル ヘディンガムで生まれた時，父John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxfordは27歳，母Maud de Badlesmereは32歳であった。彼はAlice FitzWalterとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1400年2月15日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアオクスフォードで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスハドレイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Aubrey de Vere 10th Earl of Oxford(이)가 대략 1338년 영국 에세스 카스틀 헤딩함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Vere 7th Earl of Oxford(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Badlesmere(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Alice FitzWalter(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1400년 2월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 하들리에 묻혔다.

У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Alice FitzWalter. Он умер 15 февраля 1400 г. в местности оксфорд, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности хадлиг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
